L1.area: Artifact management and tracking server infrastructure

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where URL-encoded directory traversal sequences are not properly decoded before safety checks are applied. An attacker can use percent-encoded characters to bypass path validation, traversing outside the intended directory to access or modify arbitrary files on the server.

L3.area: URI parsing and path validation utility module used by the tracking and artifact servers
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where URL-encoded directory traversal sequences (such as percent-encoded dot-dot-slash patterns) are not decoded before path safety validation is performed. This allows an attacker to bypass the path validation check by submitting percent-encoded traversal sequences through the tracking server or artifact management API endpoints, gaining access to arbitrary files on the server.

ground_truth.reason: The path validation function does not decode URL-encoded input before checking for traversal sequences, allowing attackers to bypass safety checks using percent-encoded characters like %2E%2E%2F to access arbitrary files.
